tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Nearly all tests are pure text-matching: they read file contents and assert substrings like 'assert "JSCallbackDataWeak* m_data" in h_src'. The only execution-based test (test_jsabortalgorithm_compiles_with_weak_ref) runs clang but only checks for syntax errors, not behavioral correctness. No test actually runs the code, executes the streams API, or verifies memory leak behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation that simply inserts the expected string patterns into the source files (e.g., adding comments containing 'm_abortAlgorithms', 'JSCallbackDataWeak* m_data', etc.) would pass all the text-matching tests without actually fixing the memory leak. The tests don't verify runtime behavior.

behavior_in_tests.reason: instruction.md says 'When all JavaScript references to an AbortSignal are dropped and the pipe never completes, the AbortSignal should be garbage collected.' No test verifies this actual GC behavior — all tests only check source code text patterns.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (e.g., test_jsabortalgorithm_compiles_with_weak_ref, test_abort_signal_visit_abort_algorithms_implemented), grouped into gates vs fail-to-pass vs pass-to-pass sections, and have docstrings explaining their purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves C++ source code modifications, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths, variable names, and function names in the instruction and tests appear correct and consistent with the codebase.
